title: abs
summary: Returns the absolute value of the supplied number.
details: Use when you need magnitude without preserving the original sign.

title: avg
summary: Returns the arithmetic average of the supplied sequence.
details: The input must be a comparable numeric or duration-like sequence.

title: current-date
summary: Returns the current date from the dynamic context.
details: The value is stable for the duration of a single transformation or query evaluation.

title: current-group
summary: Returns the members of the current xsl:for-each-group group.
details: It is only meaningful while group processing is active.

title: current-grouping-key
summary: Returns the grouping key for the current xsl:for-each-group iteration.
details: Use it to label or branch on the active group.

title: distinct-values
summary: Removes duplicate atomic values from a sequence.
details: The result order is implementation-dependent unless you sort afterward.

title: empty
summary: Returns true when the sequence is empty.
details: Use it for guard conditions before indexing or aggregation.

title: exists
summary: Returns true when the sequence contains at least one item.
details: It is the direct inverse of empty().

title: filter
signature: fn:filter($input as item()*, $predicate as function(item()) as xs:boolean) as item()*
summary: Applies a predicate function to each item and keeps the matching items.
details: This is useful for functional pipelines over sequences.

title: fold-left
signature: fn:fold-left($input as item()*, $zero as item()*, $action as function(item()*, item()) as item()*) as item()*
summary: Reduces a sequence from left to right using an accumulator function.
details: Use it to derive a single summary value or build a complex result.

title: for-each
signature: fn:for-each($input as item()*, $action as function(item()) as item()*) as item()*
summary: Applies a function to every item in order.
details: This is the functional equivalent of a simple mapping loop.

title: format-date
signature: fn:format-date($value as xs:date?, $picture as xs:string) as xs:string?
summary: Formats a date using an XSLT picture string.
details: The picture controls zero padding, language-sensitive rendering, and calendar details.

title: format-dateTime
signature: fn:format-dateTime($value as xs:dateTime?, $picture as xs:string) as xs:string?
summary: Formats a dateTime using an XSLT picture string.
details: Use it when rendering timestamps for human-readable output.

title: format-number
signature: fn:format-number($value as numeric?, $picture as xs:string) as xs:string
summary: Formats a numeric value according to a decimal-format picture.
details: Named decimal formats can further control separators and symbols.

title: head
summary: Returns the first item in a sequence.
details: It is a concise alternative to $seq[1].

title: innermost
summary: Returns only nodes that are not ancestors of any other supplied node.
details: Use it to remove redundant ancestors from a node set.

title: json-to-xml
summary: Parses JSON text into the XPath/XSLT XML representation of JSON.
details: It is often paired with xml-to-json() for round-tripping.

title: lower-case
summary: Converts alphabetic characters to lower case.
details: The conversion follows Unicode case rules.

title: map:contains
signature: map:contains($map as map(*), $key as xs:anyAtomicType) as xs:boolean
summary: Returns true when the map contains the supplied key.
details: It does not retrieve the value; use map:get() for that.

title: map:get
signature: map:get($map as map(*), $key as xs:anyAtomicType) as item()*
summary: Returns the value associated with a key in a map.
details: An absent key returns the empty sequence.

title: map:keys
summary: Returns the sequence of keys present in the map.
details: Sort the result when you need stable output order.

title: matches
signature: fn:matches($input as xs:string?, $pattern as xs:string) as xs:boolean
summary: Tests whether a string matches a regular expression.
details: XPath regular expressions are XML Schema regexes with XPath/XSLT extensions.

title: math:pi
summary: Returns the mathematical constant pi.
details: Useful for trigonometric calculations in XPath and XSLT.

title: math:sqrt
summary: Returns the square root of the supplied number.
details: Negative values yield NaN under the XPath math rules.

title: normalize-space
summary: Trims leading and trailing whitespace and collapses internal whitespace runs to single spaces.
details: Use it when preparing display strings or normalized keys.

title: outermost
summary: Returns only nodes that are not descendants of any other supplied node.
details: Use it to eliminate nested duplicates while keeping the highest nodes.

title: parse-json
summary: Parses JSON text into maps, arrays, and atomic values.
details: This is the direct functional counterpart to json-to-xml().

title: path
summary: Returns a stable path-style string for a node.
details: This is mainly useful for diagnostics and trace output.

title: QName
signature: fn:QName($uri as xs:string?, $lexical as xs:string) as xs:QName
summary: Creates an xs:QName value from a namespace URI and lexical QName.
details: Use it when a QName must be constructed dynamically.

title: replace
signature: fn:replace($input as xs:string?, $pattern as xs:string, $replacement as xs:string) as xs:string
summary: Replaces substrings that match a regular expression.
details: The replacement string supports regex group references.

title: resolve-uri
signature: fn:resolve-uri($relative as xs:string?, $base as xs:string?) as xs:anyURI?
summary: Resolves a relative URI against a base URI.
details: This is useful for stylesheet, document, and resource resolution logic.

title: reverse
summary: Returns the sequence in reverse order.
details: The items themselves are unchanged.

title: round-half-to-even
signature: fn:round-half-to-even($value as numeric?, $precision as xs:integer?) as numeric?
summary: Rounds numbers using banker's rounding.
details: This is often preferred for financial-style repeated rounding.

title: serialize
signature: fn:serialize($input as item()*, $params as item()?) as xs:string
summary: Serializes a value or tree using serialization parameters.
details: In XSLT this is useful for diagnostics or adaptive text output.

title: sort
summary: Returns a sorted sequence.
details: Optional collation and key function arguments control locale and sort criteria.

title: static-base-uri
summary: Returns the static base URI in the current expression context.
details: Use it when resolving resources relative to the containing stylesheet module.

title: string-join
signature: fn:string-join($values as xs:anyAtomicType*, $separator as xs:string) as xs:string
summary: Joins items into one string with a separator between each item.
details: Items are atomized and converted to strings before joining.

title: subsequence
signature: fn:subsequence($input as item()*, $start as xs:double, $length as xs:double?) as item()*
summary: Returns a slice of a sequence starting at a 1-based position.
details: Use it for paging, trimming, and windowing sequence values.

title: tail
summary: Returns all items except the first one.
details: It is the complement of head() for simple recursive or functional flows.

title: tokenize
signature: fn:tokenize($input as xs:string?, $pattern as xs:string) as xs:string*
summary: Splits a string into tokens using a regular expression separator.
details: This is frequently used for delimited text processing.

title: upper-case
summary: Converts alphabetic characters to upper case.
details: The conversion follows Unicode case rules.

title: xml-to-json
summary: Serializes the XML representation of JSON back to JSON text.
details: Pair it with json-to-xml() for transformations between models.

title: array:append
signature: array:append($array as array(*), $member as item()*) as array(*)
summary: Returns a new array with the supplied member appended.
details: Arrays are immutable, so the original value is unchanged.

title: array:head
summary: Returns the first member of an array.
details: Use it for functional array processing.

title: array:size
summary: Returns the number of members in an array.
details: It is the array equivalent of sequence counting.

title: array:tail
summary: Returns a new array without its first member.
details: Use it alongside array:head() for recursive array algorithms.
